steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The left side is a complete independent clause. The right side names specific researchers and their contributions — it elaborates on who the multiple researchers are. The punctuation must introduce that elaboration and allow while to contrast Jesus Rivera's listing position with the other coauthors.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'semicolon': Treats the second clause as equally independent — loses the elaborative relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — 'comma + while': A comma is too weak to signal that what follows explains the main clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'period': Splits closely related ideas into separate sentences unnecessarily. ✗
  • Choice D — 'colon + while': The colon signals elaboration (answers which researchers?); while then shows Rivera is listed first and others also contributed — together justifying multiple researchers. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
